svg.text: Full data conversion chain: ScheduleBatch → ModelWorkerBatch → ForwardBatch

g.text: LEVEL 1 · ScheduleBatch (Scheduler)
ScheduleBatch
managers/schedule_batch.py
Batch built by Scheduler, contains:
• reqs: List[Req] — list of request objects
• input_ids, positions, req_pool_indices — numpy arrays
Host-side data structure used for scheduling decisions and KV Cache management
LEVEL 2 · ModelWorkerBatch (TP Worker)
ModelWorkerBatch.from_schedule_batch(schedule_batch)
managers/schedule_batch.py
Conversion operations:
• Add sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo — sampling params (temperature, top_p, top_k, ...)
• Add lora_ids: List[str] — LoRA Adapter IDs
• Add capture_hidden_mode: CaptureHiddenMode — Hidden State capture mode
• Add input_embedding / deepstack_visual_embedding — Multimodal inputs
Still Host-side numpy arrays, but now contains all params required for model execution
TP Worker holds ModelWorkerBatch, used to invoke ModelRunner
LEVEL 3 · ForwardBatch (ModelRunner)
ForwardBatch.init_new(batch, model_runner)
model_executor/forward_batch_info.py
Convert to JAX pytree (device-side data structure):
• input_ids: jax.Array — converted from numpy to JAX Array, transferred to device
• positions: jax.Array — Position IDs
• req_to_token_pool: ReqToTokenPool — KV Cache index map (Host-side)
• attn_backend: AttentionBackend — Attention impl (FlashAttention / MLA / Native)
• sampling_info: SamplingBatchInfo — sampling params
Note: memory_pools is not a ForwardBatch field; ModelRunner passes it to JIT separately (donate_argnames)
Registered with @register_pytree_node_class, can be passed directly to JIT-compiled functions
Device data (JAX Array) and Host metadata (numpy) coexist, unified via pytree mechanism
STEP 4 · Model Forward
model(forward_batch.input_ids, forward_batch.positions, forward_batch)
models/*.py (e.g. llama.py, qwen2.py)
• JIT-compiled model function, accepts ForwardBatch as parameter
• Model computes layer-by-layer, reads/writes KV Cache via forward_batch.attn_backend.forward()
• Returns logits: jax.Array (shape: [batch_size, vocab_size])
Key Design Notes
Why three levels of Batch?
• ScheduleBatch: Scheduler view, focuses on request management and KV Cache allocation
• ModelWorkerBatch: TP Worker view, adds sampling params and LoRA info needed for model execution
• ForwardBatch: ModelRunner view, converted to JAX pytree, can be passed directly to JIT-compiled functions
JAX pytree Mechanism
• JAX requires JIT inputs to be pytrees (nested dict/list/tuple + JAX Array/numpy)
• ForwardBatch is registered as a pytree via @register_pytree_node_class
• Implements tree_flatten() and tree_unflatten() to define serialization/deserialization
Host-Device Data Transfer
• numpy array → JAX Array: automatically transferred to device (TPU)
• req_to_token_pool stays on Host (numpy) since Scheduler needs to read/write frequently
• memory_pools on device (JAX Array), passed to JIT by ModelRunner via donate_argnames
Shape Bucketing
• All arrays padded to predefined bucket sizes (e.g. [32, 64, 128, 256, ...])
• Avoids re-JIT compilation when batch size changes
• real_bs field records actual batch size for masking invalid data
Attention Backend Abstraction
• ForwardBatch holds reference to attn_backend
• Model layer calls attn_backend.forward(layer, q, k, v, forward_batch)
• Backend reads/writes KV Cache, performs Attention computation (FlashAttention / MLA / Native)
Data Flow Summary
Scheduler (Host) → TP Worker (Host) → ModelRunner (Host → TPU) → Model (TPU)
Each level adds info needed by that layer, ultimately forming a directly executable device-side structure
This layered design separates concerns: each component only handles its own data conversion
